questions.question: What percentage of your body's serotonin is produced in the gut?
questions.explanation: Your gut produces over 90% of your body's serotonin, a key neurotransmitter for happiness and well-being. This is why gut health is so closely linked to mood.

questions.question: What is the vagus nerve's primary role in the gut-brain connection?

options.text: It digests food in the stomach
It serves as a direct communication highway between the gut and the brain
It produces antibodies to fight infection
It stores vitamins and minerals for later use

questions.explanation: The vagus nerve is the longest cranial nerve in your body and acts as the main highway for two-way communication between your gut and your brain, carrying about 80% of messages from gut to brain.

questions.question: Approximately what percentage of vagus nerve signals travel FROM the gut TO the brain?
questions.explanation: About 80% of the messages traveling through the vagus nerve go FROM your gut TO your brain, meaning your gut has a major influence on how your brain processes mood and stress.

questions.question: How many neurons does the gut contain?
questions.explanation: Your gut contains over 500 million neurons -- more than your spinal cord. This enteric nervous system produces many of the same neurotransmitters as your brain.

questions.question: Which activity can help activate the vagus nerve and strengthen the gut-brain connection?

options.text: Skipping meals to rest the gut

questions.explanation: Deep breathing exercises activate the vagus nerve, which strengthens the gut-brain connection. This is one of the simplest daily practices for improving both gut and mental health.

title: Your Gut as Your Second Brain - Quiz
